subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, ADB, WHO, Freedom House, RSF, TI, Lowy) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.
Resource-rich but governance-poor 'paradox of plenty' - LNG/gold/copper wealth coexists with a CPI of 26/100 and chronic FX shortages now easing under an IMF-backed crawl.

Standout drags: extreme security/human-development failure (the Jan 2024 'Black Wednesday' riots, the Feb 2024 Enga massacre, among the world's worst crime rates, a 2025 polio outbreak), against an IMF reform anchor and Western security alignment.

economic.notes: An IMF-anchored consolidation and easing FX rationing, against a resource-curse base.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit narrowed to ~2.4% of GDP (2025) targeting balance by 2027 under an IMF ECF/EFF program; debt elevated but on a consolidation path. (IMF 2025-26)

monetary_independence.notes: Central-bank governance strengthened under the IMF program and the kina shifted to a crawl-like arrangement, easing FX rationing, with credibility still rebuilding. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The kina is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and managed (~0.6% in 2024, rebounding to ~3.8% in 2025). (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: Dominated by BSP Financial Group - broadly stable but shallow and concentrated, historically FX-constrained. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A large extractive base (LNG, gold, copper; Porgera restart, pending Papua LNG/P'nyang) but a textbook resource curse where revenue rarely reaches the broad economy. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: An independent-but-weakly-enforced judiciary, systemic corruption, thin data, and a deteriorating press.

rule_of_law.notes: The judiciary retains some independence but enforcement is weak and corruption systemic, with CPI at 26/100 (142nd). (Freedom House 2025; TI 2024)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak data systems - even health/crime figures are acknowledged undercounts. (WHO PNG 2025; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Limited administrative reach and patronage-prone - the Black Wednesday trigger (a payroll error cutting police pay) exposed core failures. (Freedom House 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Mostly free media but a sharply falling RSF rank and a proposed media law would let authorities sanction journalists. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Electoral democracy with unstable coalitions, severe and worsening communal violence, and a youth bulge.

political_stability.notes: Electoral democracy with peaceful transfers but unstable coalitions; the 2025 constitutional amendment buys stability by curbing the no-confidence mechanism. (Freedom House 2025; ANU)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly free speech/assembly/religion, but recurring states of emergency and weak due process. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic exit-ban regime, but high crime makes foreigners targets and administration is weak. (DFAT; inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Severe and worsening - the Jan 2024 riots (22+ dead) plus escalating highlands tribal warfare (the Feb 2024 Enga massacre ~44+). (Al Jazeera; Time; BenarNews)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a potential dividend but currently a jobs/services burden fuelling urban crime. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A Western-tilted security partner with no interstate conflict and easing capital controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: Anchored to Australia (a bilateral security treaty) and the US (a 2023 Defense Cooperation Agreement) as the West counters China. (Lowy; CRS)

conflict_involvement.notes: No interstate conflict; the 'conflict' is internal tribal/communal violence captured under social cohesion. (international press)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned, but FX rationing has functioned as a de-facto capital control for years (now easing under the IMF roadmap). (IMF 2025)

physical_practical.notes: Among the region's weakest health and worst crime, with high natural-hazard exposure.

climate.notes: Carteret Islanders among the first climate-displaced; highland landslides/floods, very low adaptive capacity, high disaster exposure. (IPCC AR6; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Among the region's weakest - a 2025 polio outbreak, high maternal mortality (~171/100k, likely understated), recurrent measles/TB. (WHO PNG; UNICEF)

infrastructure.notes: Poor roads, an unreliable grid, and limited water/broadband, with much of the highlands road-inaccessible. (World Bank; ADB)

crime_safety.notes: Among the worst globally - severe violent and gang ('raskol') crime in Port Moresby, Lae, and Mt Hagen. (DFAT 2025; international press)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against seismic, volcanic, and hydrometeorological hazard. (inference)

summary: The archetypal resource-curse state - substantial LNG, gold, and copper wealth (Porgera restarted, Papua LNG/P'nyang pending) sitting atop weak institutions (CPI 26/100), chronic FX shortages now easing under a credible IMF program, and some of the world's worst security and health outcomes. 2024 was a violent inflection: the January 'Black Wednesday' riots (22+ dead) and the February Enga massacre (~44+) underscored escalating violence, while a 2025 polio outbreak and high maternal mortality signal deep human-development failure. Politics is superficially stabilizing - Marape entrenched via an 18-month no-confidence immunity - but that stability is bought by weakening checks. The skew is negative: the IMF reform story is genuine upside, but dwarfed by correlated downside in security, health, governance, and disaster exposure.
